meta.source: Expert reference itinerary (LLM-authored from knowledge — treat as informed human, not verified personal trip)
meta.confidence: high — core timing and neighborhood logic is well-established; specific restaurant recommendations are lower confidence
meta.base_camp: Higashiyama / Gion area — ideally within walking distance of Hanamikoji Street
meta.notes: Single base in Higashiyama. All major sites within 30 min by bus or 45 min by bike. Arashiyama requires a dedicated day trip (~45 min each way by train).

locations_visited.name: Fushimi Inari Taisha
locations_visited.note: Arrive before 07:00 — the thousands of torii gates are empty at dawn, wall-to-wall tourists by 10 AM. Hike at least to the first summit (Yotsutsuji intersection) for the city view.

locations_visited.name: Kiyomizudera Temple
locations_visited.note: Go at opening (06:00 in spring) or late afternoon. The wooden stage view is the defining image of Kyoto.

locations_visited.name: Ninenzaka and Sannenzaka lanes
locations_visited.note: The preserved stone-paved lanes below Kiyomizudera. Morning only — by 10 AM packed with tour groups.

locations_visited.name: Hanamikoji Street, Gion
locations_visited.note: Visit at dusk (17:00–19:00). Highest probability of spotting geiko or maiko leaving ochaya. Do not block alleys or chase them — this is their workplace.

locations_visited.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
locations_visited.note: 06:00–07:30 ONLY. By 09:00 it is a tourist conveyor belt. This is non-negotiable — there is no other time.

locations_visited.name: Tenryu-ji Temple and Garden
locations_visited.note: Open 08:30. The Sogenchi garden is one of Japan's finest. Go right at opening before tour buses.

locations_visited.name: Togetsukyo Bridge, Arashiyama
locations_visited.note: Morning mist over the bridge and Arashiyama mountain behind. 30 min is enough.

locations_visited.name: Jojakko-ji Temple
locations_visited.note: The hidden pagoda above Arashiyama — most tourists miss it. 15-min walk up from Bamboo Grove, almost always quiet.

locations_visited.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
locations_visited.note: Opens 09:00. Arrive at 09:00 sharp — the site fills fast. The reflection in the Kyoko-chi pond is the shot. 45 min is enough.

locations_visited.name: Ryoan-ji Temple (rock garden)
locations_visited.note: Best experienced in silence, which requires arriving before 09:30. The 15 stones are arranged so you can never see all 15 at once from any angle — the garden is a puzzle, not a decoration.

locations_visited.name: Nijo Castle
locations_visited.note: The nightingale floors (uguisubari) that chirp underfoot to detect intruders are the main draw. Allow 1.5 hrs.

locations_visited.name: Philosopher's Path (Tetsugaku-no-Michi)
locations_visited.note: The 2km canal walk lined with cherry trees. In April, peak blossom. Best in the morning before crowds arrive at 10 AM.

locations_visited.name: Nishiki Market
locations_visited.note: Kyoto's kitchen — 400-year-old covered market. Best 10:00–13:00 on weekdays. Eat as you walk: pickles, skewered tofu, tamagoyaki.

locations_visited.name: Pontocho Alley
locations_visited.note: Narrow lantern-lit alley running parallel to Kamogawa. Dinner here at 19:00–21:00 is the quintessential Kyoto evening. Reserve ahead — the best restaurants fill quickly.

locations_notably_excluded.name: Kinkakuji afternoon visit
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Crowds are unmanageable after 10 AM — only worth visiting at opening

locations_notably_excluded.name: Bamboo Grove after 9 AM
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Literally not the same experience — tourist volume destroys the atmosphere

locations_notably_excluded.name: Arashiyama + Higashiyama same day
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Opposite ends of the city, ~1 hr apart. Doing both means rushing both.

locations_notably_excluded.name: Fushimi Inari midday
locations_notably_excluded.reason: 30°C+ heat and solid tourist traffic make the hike miserable — dawn only

days.title: Arrival in Kyoto
days.theme: Travel and orientation. One gentle evening activity maximum.

rows.notes: Take subway or taxi to accommodation in Higashiyama

rows.activity: Evening stroll — Hanamikoji Street and Gion
rows.notes: Gentle introduction to the neighborhood. Dusk is the best time anyway — geiko hour.
Stay local — don't travel far on arrival night

day_principles: Arrival evening should be geographically local and low-effort.
Hanamikoji at dusk is high-value and requires no energy — perfect for tired travelers.
Do not plan an ambitious arrival evening — travel fatigue is real.

days.title: Eastern Kyoto — Fushimi + Higashiyama
days.theme: Pre-dawn start for Fushimi Inari, then Higashiyama temples, Gion at dusk.

rows.notes: Train from Tofukuji or Inari Station — 20 min from Higashiyama

rows.activity: Fushimi Inari Taisha — hike to Yotsutsuji
rows.notes: Dawn light, empty tunnels. Hike to first summit for city view. Return before crowds.
Rest and eat before the next wave
Worth seeing even mid-morning. Ninenzaka and Sannenzaka lanes below.
Yudofu (tofu hot pot) at a local restaurant near the lanes
Critical recovery — 05:30 start is exhausting
Golden hour walk. Best geiko/maiko sighting window.

day_principles: Fushimi Inari requires a pre-dawn start. This is the entire day's anchor.
After a 05:30 start and a hike, afternoon rest is not optional.
Gion dusk is a second anchor for the same day — low effort, high reward, geographically adjacent to base.

days.title: Arashiyama — Full Day
days.theme: Bamboo at dawn, then Arashiyama all day. Do not mix with other districts.

rows.notes: Sagano/San-in Line from Kyoto Station or Saga-Arashiyama Station — 30 min
Empty. Walk slowly. This is the only window.
Morning mist, mountain reflection — 30 min is enough
Opens at 08:30. The Sogenchi stroll garden.
15-min walk uphill — most visitors miss it. Moss-covered pagoda, peaceful.
Tofu cuisine or soba near Togetsukyo. Shigetsu at Tenryu-ji if budget allows.
Sit by the river. Do not rush back. This is a full-day destination.
30-min traditional boat through Hozu Gorge. Low effort, high mood.
~45 min back

day_principles: Arashiyama is a full-day destination — do not combine with Higashiyama or Northern Kyoto.
Bamboo Grove at 06:15 is the non-negotiable anchor. The rest of the day follows from it.
Afternoon rest in Arashiyama (by the river) is how you avoid the 5 PM rush on trains back.
Jojakko-ji is the hidden gem — include it or you've done the tourist version.

days.title: Northern Kyoto — Temples and Castle
days.theme: Kinkakuji at opening, Ryoanji, Nijo Castle. Mid-morning start, balanced day.

rows.notes: Arrive at opening. Reflection in Kyoko-chi pond. 45 min is plenty — don't linger.
10 min walk from Kinkakuji. Arrive before 10 AM for quiet. Sit in front of the rock garden for 15 minutes — let the arrangement reveal itself.
Small temples, local shops — unhurried
Many good options near the sites
Nightingale floors, Ninomaru Palace. Allow 1.5–2 hrs.

day_principles: Kinkakuji and Ryoanji are a natural pairing — 10 min apart, both benefit from early arrival.
This is the easiest day — no dawn start, no long hike. Use it for recovery.
Nijo Castle is an afternoon activity — doesn't require prime early timing.

days.title: Philosopher's Path + Central Kyoto
days.theme: Morning canal walk, market at midday, Pontocho farewell dinner.

rows.notes: Walk north to south. In April, cherry blossoms line the canal. Before 10 AM — peaceful.
At the south end of Philosopher's Path. The brick aqueduct inside the grounds surprises everyone.
Eat as you walk — pickled vegetables, dashimaki tamago, warabi mochi. Go hungry.
Last afternoon — unscheduled is fine
Reserve in advance. Kaiseki or creative Japanese. The setting is worth the splurge.

day_principles: Philosopher's Path is most beautiful in morning light with fewer people.
Nishiki Market is a walking lunch — don't sit down for a separate meal beforehand.
Pontocho farewell dinner is the evening anchor — reserve ahead, it's the trip's high note.

days.title: Departure

rows.activity: Breakfast at accommodation or Kyoto Station
Checkout and travel to Kyoto Station
rows.notes: 20 min by subway or taxi from Higashiyama
Allow full 3 hours from checkout to departure for luggage storage, station navigation, and platform time

day_principles: Kyoto Station is large and complex. Allow more time than you think.
Departure day is not a morning to squeeze in Nishiki Market or one more temple.
Luggage forwarding (takuhaibin) should be arranged the day before if needed.

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: Dawn is the only viable window for iconic overcrowded sites
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove, Fushimi Inari, and Kiyomizudera are utterly different experiences at 06:00 vs 10:00. The crowds don't just reduce quality — they eliminate the experience entirely. These sites require pre-dawn starts as non-negotiable anchors.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI planners tend to schedule these at 09:00–10:00 because that's a 'morning' start. The difference between 06:00 and 09:00 at Arashiyama is the difference between a spiritual experience and a theme park queue.

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: Kyoto's districts are full-day zones — never mix two districts
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: Arashiyama, Higashiyama, and Northern Kyoto (Kinkakuji area) are each 30–45 minutes apart. Combining any two means 1.5 hrs minimum of dead transit time, plus both feel rushed. Each deserves its own day.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI planners often mix Arashiyama + Higashiyama or Kinkakuji + Fushimi Inari on the same day, which destroys both.

key_principles_the_human_applied.description: A 05:30 departure after a late travel arrival the day before accumulates fatigue fast. The afternoon block (14:00–15:30) on Day 1 is essential. A tired Kyoto traveler stops appreciating the subtlety of what they're seeing.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: Same as Yellowstone — AI packs the afternoon after an early morning start.

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: Dusk anchors are as important as dawn anchors
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: Hanamikoji Street (Gion) at 17:00–19:00 and Pontocho at 19:00 are evening anchors that define the trip as much as the temples. Both require being in the right place at the right time — not squeezed in after a full afternoon.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI planners fill evenings with more sightseeing rather than treating evening anchors as first-class scheduling constraints.

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: The hidden layer beats the famous layer
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: Jojakko-ji above Arashiyama, Nanzen-ji's brick aqueduct, the Yotsutsuji viewpoint at Fushimi — these are what separate a Kyoto trip from a Kyoto checklist. They are close to the famous sites but require knowing to look.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI boards include famous sites but may miss the adjacent hidden gem that locals know. The local_tip field should always answer: 'what do visitors who didn't do their research miss here?'

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove scheduled before 07:30. Fushimi Inari scheduled before 07:00. Any iconic site with heavy crowds scheduled at opening or before.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove or Fushimi Inari scheduled after 09:00.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Each day stays within one geographic district. Arashiyama and Higashiyama on separate days. Northern Kyoto (Kinkakuji) on its own day.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Arashiyama + any Higashiyama site on the same day. Kinkakuji + Fushimi Inari on same day.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Gion/Hanamikoji scheduled at 17:00–19:00 at least once. Pontocho or evening alley dining scheduled at 19:00+.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Gion scheduled at 14:00 or Pontocho completely absent.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: At least one unscheduled afternoon block on days starting before 07:00.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Activities scheduled back-to-back on days with 05:30–06:00 starts.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: At least one non-obvious experience included (Jojakko-ji, Nanzen-ji aqueduct, Yotsutsuji viewpoint, specific street in Gion, etc.).
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Only the famous top-10 sites, nothing that shows destination depth.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: No sightseeing on departure morning. Checkout + station navigation only.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Temple or market visit scheduled on departure morning.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: At least one Kyoto-specific meal experience named (kaiseki, yudofu, Nishiki Market food walk, soba near temples).
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Generic 'Japanese restaurant' or no Kyoto food identity in any meal row.
